title: HTTP API Resources, Representations, Errors, and Handler Layers · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A public-interest newsroom incrementally builds consistent feed-reader HTTP API from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, and PostgreSQL fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP-client, HTTP-server, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of consistent feed-reader HTTP API while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Design tenant, source, subscription, entry, collection, search, and reader-state resources around client decisions rather than tables.
Separate routing, authority, request admission, application command, repository, representation, and observability with narrow interfaces.
Apply safe and idempotent method semantics, success statuses, preconditions, redirects, and retry guidance consistently.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Resource Model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 9761 during a multilingual university archive import. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile resource semantics, layered handlers, problem details, cancellation, and response finality; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Design tenant, source, subscription, entry, collection, search, and reader-state resources around client decisions rather than tables.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Resource Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for design tenant, source, subscription, entry, collection, search, and reader-state resources around client decisions rather than tables. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Resource Model, keep Feed Go Resource Model connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

options.text: A CRUD endpoint for every table is a coherent API. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.

For commit to the mission model, import one convenient trusted RSS feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, XML and HTTP budgets, PostgreSQL constraints and transaction, schedule, lease, goroutine cleanup, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as feed-service proof.
State the Feed Go Resource Model preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
